mechanism: Proposed actions include HPA-axis modulation and anti-inflammatory signaling; these mechanisms remain partly inferential across study designs.
effects: Randomized controlled trial evidence suggests improvements in stress outcomes in some adults.
contraindications: Avoid in pregnancy unless supervised by qualified clinicians.
safetyNotes: Short-term studies suggest tolerability, but real-world products vary in concentration and quality.
description: Withania somnifera is the scientific name for ashwagandha and is used in Ayurvedic practice and modern standardized supplements.
dosage: Clinical dosing is extract-specific and cannot be generalized across powders, teas, and concentrated products.
interactions: Monitor potential interactions with sedatives and thyroid-related medications.
preparation: Typically prepared as root powder or standardized root extract capsules/tablets.
region: Originates in South Asia and is now marketed worldwide.
traditionalUse: Traditional Ayurvedic use includes vitality, sleep, and stress resilience support.
summary: Withania somnifera has moderate randomized evidence for stress-related symptom improvements in standardized extracts, with unresolved long-term safety and interaction questions.
